title: The Storm Tower
description: A sorcerer's tower only appears during thunderstorms. Your crew has one night to climb it, steal the Heartstone, and escape before the lightning stops.
author: AI

intro.passage: Thunder splits the sky and the Storm Tower appears on the hilltop, stone by stone, conjured from lightning. Your crew crouches in the wet grass below. You spot a ground-floor door, rough outer walls to climb, and a cellar grate at the base. The tower only stands while the storm lasts.

ground-door.passage: The iron door swings open at your touch. No lock, no resistance. That worries you. A long corridor stretches ahead, lit by candles that float in midair. The floor tiles alternate black and white in a precise pattern. Kael kneels and studies them. The black ones are pressure plates.

outer-wall.passage: Rain hammers you as you scale the tower's rough stone face. Lightning turns night to day in sharp flashes. Thirty feet up you find a dark window with no glass. Fifty feet higher you see another, faintly lit from within. The stone is slick but the mortar gaps give good holds.

cellar-grate.passage: A rusted iron grate sits half-hidden in the hillside below the tower's foundation. Kael works the bolts free with practiced hands while rain runs down your backs. The grate lifts to reveal a narrow tunnel that slopes downward into darkness. The air rising from below smells of sulfur and dried herbs.

tile-trap.passage: You test a black tile with your boot. It sinks with a click. Darts whistle from slots in the walls. You throw yourself flat as they pass overhead. One grazes your sleeve. Kael pulls you to your feet. Beyond the trapped section, a cluttered workshop opens up. Stairs rise in the back corner.

safe-tiles.passage: You step only on the white tiles, hopping across the pattern like a careful dance. Kael follows your lead. The corridor ends and opens into a cluttered workshop. Unfinished constructs stand along the walls like sleeping soldiers. A stone staircase rises at the far end. The tower hums with storm energy.

library.passage: You pull yourself through the dark window into a circular room lined with bookshelves. Scrolls and leather volumes fill every surface. A construct of bronze and glass stands motionless in the corner, its eyes dark. A spiral staircase in the center leads both up and down through the tower's core.

upper-window.passage: You haul yourself through a window three stories up into a small bedroom. Simple furnishings, a narrow bed, a writing desk. A leather journal sits on the nightstand, its pages marked with a ribbon. The stairs continue upward through an open hatch. Thunder shakes the walls and rain drives sideways through the glass.

alchemy-lab.passage: The tunnel opens into a low-ceilinged lab. Shelves line every wall, packed with jars of colored powder and bubbling flasks. A stone cauldron simmers in the center. You spot a workbench with tools and rows of labeled vials. Stairs lead up to a workshop above. A central stairwell rises beyond.

workshop.passage: Half-built constructs stand in rows along the walls, bronze limbs and glass eyes waiting to be assembled. One is nearly complete, a soldier-shaped thing that lacks only a spark of power. Plans on the workbench show dozens more. A stairway rises in the corner. The sorcerer is building an army.

stairwell.passage: A spiral staircase winds up through the tower's hollow core. Thunder booms and the stone trembles beneath your feet. Lightning strobes through narrow windows, lighting the steps in sharp white flashes. You pass an upper hall and glimpse a heavy door marked Guardian Room. The Heartstone waits above. The storm will not last forever.

library-search.passage: A weathered book on storm magic describes the Heartstone. It rests in the crown chamber at the tower's peak, held by a ward keyed to the sorcerer's will. The book mentions a bypass. Any substance that dissolves magical bonds can break the ward. The crown chamber has a direct stairway from the upper hall.

sorcerer-journal.passage: The journal is written in a cramped hand. The sorcerer calls himself Aldren. He writes of the Heartstone not as a weapon but as a burden. The storms are not attacks on the farmlands. They are a release valve. Without them, the ley-line beneath the hill would burst. Papers in a side room may hold proof.

upper-hall.passage: The upper hall is a wide circular chamber with tall windows that rattle with each thunderclap. Rain streams across the glass. A heavy door leads to the guardian room where something hums with power. A narrower stairway spirals upward to the crown chamber and the Heartstone. Time presses. The storm is weakening.

guardian-room.passage: The door opens on a square chamber occupied by a bronze construct twice your height. Glass eyes glow a steady amber. It stands motionless in the center of the room, arms at its sides, but you can hear gears turning inside its chest. A side passage leads to the crown stairs beyond.

crown-approach.passage: The narrow stairway ends at a single door of white stone. Beyond it you can feel the Heartstone's pulse, deep and rhythmic like a second heartbeat layered over your own. The door is warm to the touch. Lightning flashes through a window above and for a moment the whole stairwell glows blue.

fight-guardian.passage: The construct lunges the moment you step forward. Bronze fists swing in heavy arcs. You dodge the first strike and slam your blade into the joint of its arm. Gears screech. Kael throws a flask of solvent at its chest and the metal hisses and warps. The stairs behind you offer retreat.

sneak-past.passage: You flatten against the wall and edge around the chamber while the construct stares ahead with its amber eyes. Each step is agony. A side passage leads to the crown stairs. A loose stone shifts under your foot. The construct's head rotates toward the sound. Its eyes brighten. You have a heartbeat to choose.

ward-challenge.passage: You push the door open. The crown chamber is small and round, open to the storm through a ring of arched windows. On a stone pedestal in the center, the Heartstone blazes with trapped lightning. A shimmering ward surrounds it. You reach through the field and your fingers close on warm crystal.

storm-warning.passage: You press your ear to the door. The stone vibrates with the Heartstone's power. But beneath that hum you hear the storm weakening. The thunder rolls farther apart. The rain thins. The tower shudders. When the storm dies, the tower goes with it. You have minutes at best, not hours.

alarm-triggered.passage: The construct roars to life. Bronze arms swing and its eyes flare red. An alarm bell rings somewhere above. The tower knows you are here. Kael grabs your arm and pulls you toward a side passage. The construct's footsteps shake the floor behind you. The crown chamber is ahead but so is danger.

sabotage.passage: You take Kael's knife to the workbench plans and cut the power linkages on the nearest constructs. Bronze limbs go slack. While cutting, you notice a hidden compartment in the bench. Inside is a second set of plans, personal notes, and a sealed letter addressed to the farmer's guild. The sorcerer expected you.

discovery-room.passage: The letter explains everything. Aldren the sorcerer built the tower to contain the ley-line's pressure. The storms are controlled releases that prevent disaster. If the Heartstone is removed, the ley-line will burst within a week and the blast will flatten every farm for twenty leagues. He begs whoever reads this to understand.

clean-heist-ending.passage: The Heartstone lifts free with barely a whisper. The ward dissolves like frost. You tuck the crystal into your pack and descend at a run. Kael and Wren meet you at the base. The three of you vanish into the rain as the tower crumbles to dust behind you. Clean work.

ending.title: The Perfect Lift
ending.flavor: In and out before the thunder stopped rolling.

narrow-escape-ending.passage: You wrench the Heartstone free and the tower screams. Stone cracks. The stairwell tilts. You run downward as floors collapse above you. Kael grabs your belt and hauls you through the front door as the tower dissolves into rubble and rain. You lie in the mud, gasping, the warm crystal pressed to your chest.

ending.title: By a Thread
ending.flavor: The stone is yours but the bruises will last longer.

storm-escape-ending.passage: The storm is dying. The tower groans and the walls begin to fade at the edges. You grab Kael and run for the stairs. Floors blur and dissolve beneath your boots. You leap from the crumbling doorway into the rain as the tower vanishes behind you. No stone. But you are alive.

ending.title: Empty Handed
ending.flavor: There will be other storms and the tower always comes back.

truth-ending.passage: You leave the Heartstone on its pedestal and walk out of the tower. Kael stares at you but says nothing. You will tell the farmer's guild what you found. The storms are not an attack. They are a shield. Aldren's burden is not yours to carry but neither is it yours to break.

ending.title: The Sorcerer's Burden
ending.flavor: Not every treasure is meant to be taken.

trapped-ending.passage: The construct blocks every path. You and Kael retreat as the alarm rings on. The storm fades. The walls go transparent. When the last thunder dies the tower vanishes and drops you on the bare hilltop in the rain. Wren hauls you up. The tower is gone and so is your chance.

ending.title: Dust and Rain
ending.flavor: The storm always ends and the tower always wins.
